title: High-quality processes and rare nonconformities
text: IQCC distinguishes two corrected methods: type = "cf1" uses first skewness correction; type = "cf2" uses two-adjustment operational limits documented Joekes Barbosa (2013). Use explicit names new code. historical alias "CF" remains available cchart.p() maps "cf1". pure numerical functions make underlying calculation directly inspectable:
code: library(IQCC)  data(binomdata)  cchart.p(   x1 = binomdata$Di[1:12],   n1 = binomdata$ni[1:12],   type = "cf2",   x2 = binomdata$Di[13:25],   n2 = binomdata$ni[13:25] ) lim <- pchart_limits(p = 0.015, n = 20, type = "cf2") lim  pchart_alpha_risk(   p = 0.015,   n = 20,   lcl = lim$lcl,   ucl = lim$ucl )

title: High-quality processes and rare nonconformities
text: double-sampling np chart uses two possible sampling stages. first sample inspected. count nonconforming items clearly acceptable, process signaled. clearly large, chart signals immediately. count lies intermediate region, second sample inspected combined count used final decision. DS-np method can reduce average sample size maintaining desired monitoring performance. especially relevant inspection expensive nonconformities rare. IQCC provides complete DS-np workflow: dsnp_prob_accept() computes total acceptance probability; dsnp_arl() computes average run length; dsnp_ass() computes average sample size; dsnp_limits() searches ranks feasible limits; cchart.DSnp() classifies observations constructs chart.

title: High-quality processes and rare nonconformities
text: DS-np plan first-stage count D1 second-stage count D2, IQCC uses following rule: accept first stage D1 <= floor(wl); signal first stage D1 >= floor(ucl1) + 1; otherwise inspect second sample; second sample, accept D1 + D2 <= floor(ucl2); signal second stage otherwise. wl = 1.5, ucl1 = 2.5, ucl2 = 4.5: accept first stage D1 <= 1; continue second stage D1 = 2; signal first stage D1 >= 3; accept second stage D1 + D2 <= 4.

text: IQCC implements Improved Quality Control Charts: statistical process control charts exact, corrected, standardized, simulation-based limits univariate multivariate monitoring. package motivated recurring practical problem classical Shewhart-type control charts: statistic monitored discrete, skewed, bounded, strongly non-normal, usual normal-based three-sigma limits can badly misplaced. cases, nominal false-alarm risk may differ substantially actual one. IQCC keeps familiar control-chart workflow exposing numerical functions make limits, risk, ARL, sample-size calculations reproducible testable.

title: Improved Quality Control Charts
text: Univariate control charts: X-bar, R, S, p, u charts. Improved probability limits: exact, Cornish-Fisher corrected, standardized, simulation-based limits. High-quality processes: corrected p charts double-sampling np charts rare nonconformities. Multivariate monitoring: Hotelling T² charts generalized variance charts. False-alarm diagnostics: exact binomial, Poisson, range-chart, generalized variance risk calculations available. Phase Phase II support: retrospective estimation prospective monitoring. Research-oriented numerical layer: pure functions separated plotting interfaces validation simulation studies.

code: cchart.DSnp(   x1,   n1,   n2,   p0,   x2 = NULL,   wl = NULL,   ucl1 = NULL,   ucl2 = NULL,   limits = NULL,   alpha = 0.0027,   p1 = NULL,   plot = TRUE,   ... )

title: Double-Sampling np Control Chart — cchart.DSnp
text: x1 Integer vector nonconforming counts first sample. n1 First-stage sample size (positive integer). n2 Second-stage sample size (positive integer). p0 -control nonconforming proportion. x2 Optional integer vector nonconforming counts second sample. Must length x1 provided. Use NA observations second sample taken. Required observations x1 falls intermediate (warning) zone. wl Fractional warning limit. Must less ucl1. ucl1 Fractional upper control limit first stage. Must greater wl. ucl2 Fractional upper control limit combined samples. limits Optional object returned dsnp_limits(). provided, wl, ucl1, ucl2 taken limits$best. alpha Maximum desired false alarm probability p0. Used limits need computed via dsnp_limits(). p1 Optional --control proportion. provided, performance metrics p1 included returned object. plot Logical. TRUE (default), draws control chart. FALSE, returns result object. ... Additional arguments passed plot().

title: Double-Sampling np Control Chart — cchart.DSnp
text: object class "cchart.DSnp", list following elements: call matched call. data data.frame columns index, x1,     x2, total, stage, signal. limits list fractional integer thresholds:     wl, ucl1, ucl2, wl_accept,     ucl1_reject, ucl2_accept. parameters list n1, n2, p0,     alpha, p1. performance list -control performance metrics     (arl0, ass0, p_signal0), optionally     --control metrics (arl1, ass1,     p_signal1) p1 provided.

title: Double-Sampling np Control Chart — cchart.DSnp
text: DS-np chart uses two sampling stages. first stage, sample size n1 inspected. count x1 warning limit, process accepted. x1 exceeds first upper control limit, process signals --control immediately. Otherwise, second sample size n2 inspected combined count x1 + x2 compared second upper control limit. Limits can supplied manually via wl, ucl1, ucl2, obtained pre-computed dsnp_limits() object via limits argument, computed automatically inside function neither provided. fractional limits converted integer thresholds using convention numerical core functions: wl_accept = floor(wl): accept first stage         x1 <= wl_accept. ucl1_reject = floor(ucl1) + 1: signal first stage         x1 >= ucl1_reject. ucl2_accept = floor(ucl2): accept second stage         x1 + x2 <= ucl2_accept.

title: Double-Sampling np Control Chart — cchart.DSnp
text: Joekes, S., Smrekar, M. Barbosa, E. (2015). Extending double sampling control chart non-conforming proportion high quality processes case small samples. Statistical Methodology.

title: Double-Sampling np Control Chart — cchart.DSnp
text: Daniela R. Recchia, Emanuel P. Barbosa

title: Double-Sampling np Control Chart — cchart.DSnp

code: mu <- c(5.682, 88.22) Sigma <- miscTools::symMatrix(c(3.770, -5.495, 13.53), 2) datum <- data.1(20, 10, mu, Sigma) estat <- stats(datum, 20, 10, 2) datum2 <- data.2(estat, 10, p = 2) T2II <- T2.2(datum2, estat, 10) # For the first sample j = 1. title: Double-Sampling np Chart: Acceptance Probability — dsnp_prob_accept
text: p nonconforming proportion evaluate. Can scalar numeric vector, values [0, 1]. n1 first-stage sample size (positive integer). n2 second-stage sample size (positive integer). wl fractional warning limit. Must less ucl1. ucl1 fractional upper control limit first stage. Must greater wl. ucl2 fractional upper control limit combined samples. Must greater wl.

title: Double-Sampling np Chart: Acceptance Probability — dsnp_prob_accept
text: list following elements: pa1 Probability acceptance first stage: P(D1 <= floor(wl)). pa2 Probability acceptance second stage. pt Total acceptance probability: pa1 + pa2. p_signal Total signaling probability: 1 - pt. p_decision_first Probability decision (accept signal)   made first sample. p_second Probability requiring second sample: 1 - p_decision_first. n1, n2, wl, ucl1, ucl2 input chart parameters. wl_accept Integer threshold: floor(wl). Accept D1 <= wl_accept. ucl1_reject Integer threshold: floor(ucl1) + 1. Signal D1 >= ucl1_reject. ucl2_accept Integer threshold: floor(ucl2). Accept D1 + D2 <= ucl2_accept.

title: Double-Sampling np Chart: Acceptance Probability — dsnp_prob_accept
text: chart uses two sampling stages. first stage, sample size n1 inspected. count d1 warning limit (wl), process accepted. d1 exceeds upper control limit (ucl1), process signals --control. d1 falls wl ucl1, second sample size n2 inspected combined count d1 + d2 compared ucl2.
